commit.message:
fix(api): replace router.all('/*') with router.use() — path-to-regexp v8 dropped wildcard syntax

path-to-regexp v8+ (used by router@2.2.0) requires named parameters in wildcards.
'/*' is now invalid; router.use() is the correct catch-all for stub routes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Allow CORS requests from www.metafaux.com (#42)

The frontend at `www.metafaux.com/klaxon` is blocked from loading API
data because `https://www.metafaux.com` is not in the CORS allowed
origins list.

## Changes

- **`.env.example`** — Added
`CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=https://www.metafaux.com`; production deployments
need this env var set to enable cross-origin access
- **`phpunit.xml`** — Injected `CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` for the test
environment
- **`tests/Feature/CorsTest.php`** — Added CORS tests covering:
- Preflight (`OPTIONS`) and actual `GET` requests from the allowed
origin receive the correct `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header
- Disallowed origins are not echoed back (browser will block the
request)

## How it works

`config/cors.php` already reads from `CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` via `env()`:

```php
'allowed_origins' => array_values(array_filter(array_map('trim', explode(',', env('CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS', ''))))),
```

No config changes needed — only the environment variable was missing.

test(android): create E2E test framework with Compose UI tests (#213) (#739)

* test(android): create E2E test framework with Compose UI tests (#213)

Set up end-to-end instrumented test infrastructure for the Android app:

- Custom E2ETestRunner + E2ETestApplication for isolated test env
- FakeTokenStorage pre-authenticates sessions (no network needed)
- E2ETestModule provides Koin DI with fake auth layer
- BaseE2ETest base class with createAndroidComposeRule<MainActivity>
- Robot pattern: NavigationRobot, DashboardRobot, AccountRobot
- NavigationE2ETest: 6 tests covering all bottom-nav destinations
- DashboardE2ETest: 6 tests covering summary data + accessibility
- AccountCreationE2ETest: 4 tests covering FAB -> form -> save flow
- README with running instructions and conventions

commit.message:
refactor: remove regex legal ref check, let LLM handle it

The regex legal ref whitelist kept causing false positives:
- 'Legge 78/2010' blocked because whitelist has 'DL 78/2010' (type prefix mismatch)
- 'L 207/2024' blocked because normalization stripped the dot

The LLM fact-check correctly identifies truly fabricated laws (e.g., D.Lgs 147/2015
for tassa salute) while understanding that type prefix variations of real laws aren't
fabrications. Regex pre-filter still catches fake institutions, acronyms, and known
hallucination patterns.

commit.message:
Enable runtime-async in CoreCLR System.Private.CoreLib (#126594)

## Description

Runtime-async was enabled for `src/libraries` (non-mobile/wasm) but was
missing from the CoreCLR `System.Private.CoreLib`. This adds the
`runtime-async=on` feature flag to
`src/coreclr/System.Private.CoreLib/System.Private.CoreLib.csproj`,
matching the existing NativeAOT pattern with architecture and runtime
flavor exclusions:

- **riscv64** excluded per #124934
- **loongarch64** excluded per #124935
- **Mono flavor** excluded (covers browser/wasm, wasi, android, iOS,
etc.)

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="'$(TargetArchitecture)' != 'riscv64' and '$(TargetArchitecture)' != 'loongarch64' and '$(RuntimeFlavor)' != 'Mono'">
  <Features>$(Features);runtime-async=on</Features>
</PropertyGroup>
```

commit.message:
fix: deactivate LVM/dm before partitioning; partprobe after --force --no-reread

Root cause of 'no space left on device' install failure:
- Disks previously used for LVM keep the disk open via the device-mapper layer
- sfdisk falls back to --force --no-reread (kernel rejects BLKRRPART)
- Without --no-reread recovery, the kernel retains the OLD partition table
- mkfs then formats the wrong-sized (old) partition → 930 GiB disk gets
  a 1 GiB root filesystem → bootc fills it instantly during install

Two fixes:
1. unmountAll() now calls deactivateLVM() to tear down LVM VGs and DM
   devices (LUKS, dm-crypt) backed by the target disk before sfdisk runs.
   This allows sfdisk's first attempt to succeed without --force.
2. After the --force --no-reread retry path, partprobe + udevadm settle
   is called to force the kernel to re-read the new partition table before
   any mkfs operations run.

Adds regression test: TestPartition_PartprobeAfterForceReread verifies
that partprobe is called after the --force sfdisk retry.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Field defaults to 0.001 - validators dont run on defaults

Pydantic validators only run when values are SET, not for defaults.
Changed all Field defaults from 0.0 to 0.001:
- RewardBreakdown: correctness, reasoning_quality, progress
- RewardPenalties: step_penalty, invalid_action_penalty, etc.
- Reward: early_bonus

Now model_dump() produces all values strictly in (0, 1).

commit.message:
fix(ci): replace Railway CLI with direct GraphQL API redeploy

Railway CLI redeploy requires an account token; the project deploy token
(UUID format) is rejected with 'Invalid RAILWAY_TOKEN'. The GraphQL API
accepts the deploy token for project-scoped mutations.

Hardcode all 8 service IDs and the production environment ID (queried
and verified locally). Use curl to call serviceInstanceRedeploy mutation
directly - no CLI, no npm install delay, no token format mismatch.

Service IDs sourced from project e48fdf5a-7857-4c69-a578-1ce8fa10d93e.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
